# Questions in Livestream 100

Live Stream #100: Celebration? Could Be!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=322" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:22</a> | How long have David and Kiara been married, and how did they meet? | He said they had been married 24 years and met at a science-fiction convention through a Christian gathering inside it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=389" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:29</a> | How were the Canaanites treated under Israelite rule, and how did Canaanite identity survive? | He described mixed treatment, noted that Canaanite society was already under pressure, and pointed to Phoenician expansion as the main survival line of Canaanite culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:28</a> | How reliable is Douglas Petrovich on biblical and ancient Near Eastern history from 1000 BC or after? | He said Petrovich is uneven: somewhat better on some later historical matters, but badly off the rails on early alphabetic inscriptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=682" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:22</a> | What dates does he accept for the pharaohs from Ay to Ramesses II, and what date does he give for the Exodus? | He gives Ay 1324-1320 BC, Horemheb 1320-1305 BC, Ramesses II 1288-1222 BC, and the Exodus around 1265 BC plus or minus about seven years. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=1239" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:39</a> | Why is the purification time in Leviticus 12 twice as long for a female baby? | He is not certain, but suggests circumcision may reduce the male-child period rather than the female period being doubled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=1523" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:23</a> | Why does Aaron not get punished with leprosy like Miriam in Numbers 12? | Miriam had a double offense: challenging Moses and being somewhere she should not be; Aaron did not share that second offense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=2144" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:44</a> | Does the Jordan River name come from Dan, and how would that fit Abraham or Moses? | He said the city name Dan predates the tribe and comes from a Semitic word for stronghold, so the river name is not evidence that the tribe supplied it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=2356" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:16</a> | Have Egyptologists figured out what the Set animal represents? | No. He said proposals abound, but no text or firm evidence identifies the Typhonian beast securely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=2507" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:47</a> | What is his opinion on Esther and Mordecai coming from Ishtar and Marduk? | He has no problem with it; pagan-derived names were common and do not require the people to be pagan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=2680" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:40</a> | Are there good Old Testament or ancient Near Eastern scholars who examine Messianic prophecies? | He says that work is usually done by New Testament scholars and recommends looking there, especially to D. A. Carson. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=2805" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:45</a> | What was the plague of darkness exactly? | His working hypothesis is a dark sand-and-ash storm, while noting the passage is unusual. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=3052" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:52</a> | If Moses was raised in Ramesses I's household before he became pharaoh, would Moses still qualify as a child of the nursery? | Yes. The kap was a scribal training institution, not only for royal children. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=3256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:16</a> | Why does God command a food offering in Numbers 28:2, and were Israelites feeding God? | No. The offering is about pleasing sacrifice and aroma, not God eating food as in pagan cults. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=3885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:45</a> | Did Dr. Dino prove himself to be the greatest biblical scholar and Egyptologist? | No. He identifies Dr. Dino as Kent Hovind and says historians would not take him seriously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=4265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:05</a> | Does he have another harder and rarer Hebrew word to teach? | He gives `toevah`, meaning `abomination`, and says it helps distinguish ordinary disgust from the strongest taboo category. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=4371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:51</a> | Looking back on the channel, what three things is he most thankful for? | Prayer support, financial support from viewers, and God's provision. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=4487" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:47</a> | Are there loanwords or textual evidence for dating Isaiah 40-66 before Cyrus is mentioned? | He says a late date is not required; Cyrus I and recontextualized prophecy are possible, and Benjamin Noonan is worth checking on loanwords. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=5020" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:40</a> | Are there Old Testament verses showing the Holy Spirit is God, and did Jews think so? | Genesis 1 is his main example; some Jews historically held related views, but it was not universal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=5181" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:21</a> | Did he grow his goatee to resemble a pharaonic mask? | No. Egyptian royal beards were false, and he kept the goatee because his wife liked it after he grew it while sick. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=5570" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:50</a> | How common was interest in the ancient Near East, and how should biblical usury laws be understood? | Interest was common; Israel's rules focused on not exploiting needy kin, and modern fiat and inflation contexts complicate direct application. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=6612" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:12</a> | Would the logic about living images, idols, and gods contrast with divine council theology? | He says the argument is invalid because it equivocates on `idol` and `god`. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=6759" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:39</a> | What do we currently know about Gibeon in Joshua 9:17? | He does not know the reports well enough, but notes city locations can move, so a later site does not exclude an earlier nearby city. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=6871" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:31</a> | Has he encountered Kenneth Griffith's work in Answers Research Journal, and what are his thoughts? | He is strongly critical of Griffith, Answers Research Journal scholarship, and Ussher maximalism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=7557" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:57</a> | How should biblical inerrancy be understood, and does it have limits such as copyist errors? | Inerrancy applies to the autographs and what Scripture asserts; copies can have errors, and he frames it as a dogmatic claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=8078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:38</a> | Do Genesis 10:21-31 and 11:8 confirm that Shem's descendants settled in Yemen? | A Semitic settlement in Arabian regions is plausible, but Genesis 10 is cultural anthropology, not strict genetic descent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=8361" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:21</a> | How did ancient Egyptians view sheep, and how does Exodus 8:26-27 fit? | Views changed over time; by the New Kingdom sheep could be viewed as unclean or offensive, explaining the concern about sacrifice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=9126" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:06</a> | What do Egyptian writings tell us about the literary genre of Genesis 1-11? | Egyptian literature contains many comparable genres and motifs, but Genesis 1-11 is best described broadly as mytho-history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=9437" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:17</a> | What are his thoughts about the authorship and dating of Job? | He dates Job to Iron Age II, likely from Aram or outside domestic Israel, with unknown authorship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=10072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:52</a> | What does the Hebrew expression in Psalm 29:1 mean? | He says it literally reads like `sons of the gods` or `sons of God` and likely refers to the divine council. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=10588" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:28</a> | Does he think Job was a real person? | Yes, probably, though the poetic presentation may not preserve every detail literally. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=11700" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:00</a> | Why is the name Lucifer only in the King James Version? | It comes from the Latin Vulgate as a traditional rendering of a Hebrew term meaning shining one or light bearer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=12236" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:56</a> | Why does he think Rehoboam's fifth year was Jeroboam's first year? | He follows Thiele's reconciliation; moving Jeroboam earlier breaks later synchronisms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=12400" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:40</a> | What should someone expect from a career as a Bible scholar? | It can be rewarding, but requires long graduate training, political caution, heavy publication pressure, and uncertain job prospects. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=13201" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:01</a> | If someone does not make it as a Bible scholar, then what? | Skills can transfer to nonprofits, charities, private industry, and leadership roles, but candidates must connect those skills for employers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/dE-S1eNreRU?t=13801" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:01</a> | Was Scorpion mythological or the first pharaoh or king of Egypt? | The Scorpion kings were real predynastic Upper Egyptian rulers, not mythological and not the first kings of unified Egypt. |
